subject: Product Management
title: User Interviews
description: A short lesson to help you apply User Interviews.

hook.text: The most powerful discovery tool costs nothing: sit down with a user and ask them about their life.

buildup.text: User interviews reveal problems, motivations, and workflows that no dashboard can capture.

discovery.text: The key rule: ask about past behavior, not future intentions. 'What did you do last time?' beats 'Would you use this?'

twist.text: Users are terrible at predicting their own behavior. They'll say yes to anything in an interview.

climax.text: Five well-conducted interviews reveal 85% of usability problems. You don't need a massive study — just good questions.

punchline.text: Ask what they did, not what they'd do.

quiz.question: What is the key rule for user interviews?
options: Ask about past behavior, not future intentions
Ask users to predict what features they want
Interview at least 100 users for every feature
